title: `default_subnet_pools` option doesn't support IPv6 pools
body:
<!--- Please read the [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/containers/common-files/blob/master/.github/CONTRIBUTING.md) before proceeding --->

Despite the warning in the config file, `default_subnet` works fine for IPv6 default subnets. (Note, I redacted the actual subnets here. I used actual routable IPv6 space for the test below)
```
$ grep 'default_subnet =' /etc/containers/containers.conf
default_subnet = "2001:db8::/32"

$ sudo podman network inspect default
[
     {
          "name": "default",
          "id": "37a8eec1ce19687d132fe29051dca629d164e2c4958ba141d5f4133a33f0688f",
          "driver": "bridge",
          "network_interface": "cni-podman0",
          "created": "2022-04-18T10:11:28.324707341Z",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "2001:db8::/32",
                    "gateway": "2001:db8::1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": true,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]

$ sudo podman run --rm -ti docker://alpine ping -c2 google.com  
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob df9b9388f04a done
Copying config 0ac33e5f5a done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
PING google.com (2a00:1450:4009:821::200e): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 2a00:1450:4009:821::200e: seq=0 ttl=117 time=5.489 ms
64 bytes from 2a00:1450:4009:821::200e: seq=1 ttl=117 time=5.458 ms

--- google.com ping statistics ---
2 packets transmitted, 2 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 5.458/5.473/5.489 ms
```

I would expect the newer (unreleased, as I found out in containers/common#1003) `default_subnet_pools` option to also support static IPv6 allocations. 
https://github.com/containers/common/commit/9cb71559d5051a8134a5208157a51dedad75b758#diff-f38944fdd6507cc37badbde5c66785bdebb77bd6fa2ea0674cd8117f89e95676R280-R299

This is something that Docker supports, which is saying a lot because Docker's approach to network features in containers is certainly less than sufficient.

comments.body:
I'm trying to work on implementing this, but it requires some rewriting of the "find an unused subnet" implementation because the current crude implementation works fine for IPv4 but it's so slow with IPv6 that I've not actually seen it finish yet with a _reasonable_ IPv6 subnet size.
Implementing this with Go 1.16.x compatibility in mind is a LOT harder than it would be if I could just use Go 1.18 [netip.Addr](https://pkg.go.dev/net/netip#Addr) as doing IP-level calculations on a byte slice is both slow and cumbersome. Using 128 bit integers would be much nicer.
How rigid is the Go 1.16 compatibility rule? ref https://github.com/containers/common/commit/0325c5ad733749d4a74a2df3d1bc1b49ae53a3be

I also just realised that doing IPv6 address addition is going to be near impossible with the limit of `uint64` as the largest native integer size. `netip.Addr` is implemented using a "uint128" composed of two `uint64`: https://cs.opensource.google/go/go/+/master:src/net/netip/uint128.go
It seems a shame to have to reimplement this just to keep compatibility with ancient Go versions

What's your justification for adding a new field vs using the existing field? (It always upsets me when `ipv6` gets an explicit prefix but ipv4 does not- they're equal)

I can make it work with the net.IP types, no worries.

I was testing with a pool of /32 and subnet sizes of /64 which IMO is a typical usecase

I understand that we could do one field with both subnets and separate them internally in the code. However the problem is that we have a fixed built in default for ipv4. If a user now wants to add another ipv6 subnet they now have to manually add the default ipv4 subnets too because they would get overwritten otherwise.

AFAIK default_subnet_pools should not be in a release yet, so we could rename it to default_ipv4_subnet_pools
